summary: Phase 4 BUILD for Pita Kabab (run pita-kabab-2026-07-12-b5881b47). Scaffolded the engine restaurant archetype into D:\website\pita-kabab via factory init, authored the six data collections from sealed Phase 0/1/2 evidence only, implemented the sealed Phase 3 design system (Fredoka/Nunito, warm-cream canvas, pomegranate #DC2626 CTA with #991B1B press edge, card-on-canvas, sticky call bar singleton) via a project Base.astro shadow + src/styles/tokens.css + a project-owned home page, applied the sealed fixture identityDecision exactly (domain https://pitakabab.example, email owner@pitakabab.example, gaId G-XXXXXXXXXX, paymentAccepted omitted), and ran the real factory build: exit 0, 41 static routes in dist matching the 41 unconditional approved Phase 2 routes exactly (zero missing, zero extra). The 5 owner-gated conditional pages (4 halal-gated, 1 craft-claim-gated) are FOLDED per the sealed Phase 2 plan's own conditions ('page ships only after owner confirmation' — confirmation has not arrived), executing the approved plan's conditions, not a plan change. Truthfulness floor enforced and verified against built HTML: no prices, no halal or charcoal/craft claims, no offers, no trust grid (<6 owner-confirmed facts), no lead form (no external endpoint), no sms: CTA, caveated hours, attributed identity claims. KNOWN SEAL BLOCKER: the inspect-artifact route-parity check compares routesGenerated against ALL 46 packet pageMatrix entries with no conditional-page awareness, so this artifact inspects with exactly 5 'missing approved Phase 2 route' problems for the 5 owner-gated routes; authoring those pages without owner confirmation would fabricate gated claims, so the conflict is documented in buildResult.unresolvedBuildBlockers instead of being faked green.

buildResult.routeParity: EXACT MATCH on the unconditional matrix — dist/ contains 41 index.html routes; diff against the approved Phase 2 46-route matrix reports 0 missing unconditional routes, 0 extra routes, and exactly the 5 conditional routes absent: /occasions/iftar-catering, /occasions/halal-catering, /halal-restaurant-cedar-rapids, /halal-heritage-of-cedar-rapids, /how-we-cook-charcoal-shawarma.

conditionalDropAccounting.basis: Executing the approved plan's own conditions, not a plan change: every dropped route is sealed in the Phase 2 artifact as conditional with 'page ships only after owner confirmation' (halal certification x4; charcoal/vertical-spit/scratch/generational craft claims x1). Owner confirmation has NOT arrived (owner-questionnaire.md items unanswered). The engine's fold mechanism is omitting the data entries (Phase 3 routeCompleteness: '5 conditional pages drop by removing data entries, floor 41 pages'); the 25-route workflow floor holds at 41.
conditionalDropAccounting.reinstatePath: When the owner confirms halal certification (with certifying body) and/or the craft claims, author the gated occasions.json/landings.json entries and rebuild — route count becomes 45/46 without touching any existing page.

buyerProof.home: Single h1 names the business + head term; above-the-fold subhead states what it is, where, and how to buy (dine in / take out / DoorDash); primary tel: CTA in hero, header, sticky bar; exactly-3 value-prop strip from owner-published statements (flagged owner-unconfirmed in value-props-decision.md); menu-family cards, catering-led occasions rail, CTA banner, neighborhoods + FAQ teasers, NAP block with caveated hours and directions/DoorDash links.
buyerProof.menu-category: Category hero + item cards straight from the sealed menu capture with descriptions and vegetarian tags; price column omitted entirely (owner-unverified — omit-never-fabricate); tel: CTA and CTA banner as conversion actions; cross-links to dish landings and occasions.
buyerProof.occasion: Each of the 8 occasions states the buying moment, what to order (featured items resolving to real menu entries), >=5 honest FAQs and >=4 cross-links; catering terms/pricing deliberately not republished (owner-confirm-gated) — every quote routed to the call CTA.
buyerProof.search-intent-landing: Answer-first body (2-3 sentence direct answer under the query-shaped h1), dish-definition depth sections, competitor-absence framing only where Phase 1 observed it, cross-links to parent menu category + related dish + occasion, tel: CTA; /best-mediterranean-food page is an honest guide naming observed competitors without fabricated rankings.
buyerProof.story-info-faq-landing: GEO/AI-answer pattern: question h1, direct answer above the fold, structured sections; /about ships owner-confirmed facts only (address, shared Captains ownership) with web-published claims attributed; /faq answers only from sealed evidence with hours caveat; FAQPage/AboutPage schema types set.
buyerProof.neighborhood: All 8 towns carry a city-specific h1, >=1 genuine geographic/civic local fact, >=2 town-specific proximity trust lines (HQ address line never reused as one), >=1 town FAQ, distinct bodies per town, and the labeled address keeps the HQ city; delivery answers honestly route to DoorDash coverage checks or per-event catering quotes.

sourceOrGapNotes: Phone (319) 440-3709: web-sourced from the owner site's tel: link, owner-unconfirmed — ships as the call CTA per the sealed Phase 2 conditionalBlocks.callCta rule (tel: CTA on every page, number pending canonical-phone confirmation).
sms: CTA omitted site-wide (no SMS capability evidenced; site.ctaSecondary absent — engine sticky bar renders single-CTA variant).

Hours: single-sourced to the owner site; business.hours.displayText carries the caveat 'hours as published by the restaurant — please call to confirm'; structured OpeningHoursSpecification omits Wednesday (conservative closed).
Menu prices omitted on every item (owner-unverified); Menu JSON-LD correctly not emitted by the engine (requires fully priced items).

Bowls and Kids Meals sections: sealed Phase 0 capture named no individual items — each ships one transparent section-grain entry ('Rice bowls', 'Kids meal') whose description marks the gap ('pending owner confirmation — call for today's options'); no dish names fabricated.
Trust grid omitted site-wide: 0 of the >=6 required owner-CONFIRMED facts exist (all 8 candidates unverified); site.trustBar absent, sections stack cleanly.
Lead form omitted: no external form-service endpoint provisioned; contact = phone + map + hours + directions (documented exception per plan).
site.offers omitted (kids $5.98 candidate owner-unconfirmed; no dated quantified promotion evidenced); site.financing omitted (none).

site.valueProps (3) + site.ctaBanner drafted from the owner's OWN published statements per dispatch; each line's source and 'owner-unconfirmed, requires sign-off before launch' flag recorded in value-props-decision.md.

business.yearEstablished '2025' (JSON-LD foundingDate): schema-required field with no omission path; value is the Phase 0 web-inferred opening year (address was Captains until at least May 16, 2025) — owner-unconfirmed, questionnaire item pending; swap on confirmation.

business.owner 'Ray Aqel': owner-site-published and consistent with the owner-confirmed shared-Captains-ownership brief fact; not emitted into JSON-LD and omitted from About page copy (unconfirmed-claims-omitted rule).
sameAs limited to the Yelp listing and the DoorDash store linked from the owner site; the Facebook page is excluded (ownership unverified).

brand.logoFilename logo.svg is config-only — no logo asset ships (owner asset/approval required; asset-references-exist advisory expected); imagery for all 32 Phase 2 slots is Phase 6 scope by pipeline design.
Catering terms (10-person min, 200+ capacity, 50% deposit, notice tiers) NOT republished anywhere — all catering pages quote per event via the call CTA.

Halal and craft (charcoal/vertical-spit/scratch/generational) claims appear NOWHERE in the build (dist grep-verified), including scrubbing generic 'halal-cart'/'charcoal-grilled' cuisine phrasings to eliminate association risk; 'free parking' and the 4.9/8-review rating are likewise unshipped.
Dine-in mentions are owner-site-evidenced and softened ('the restaurant's published pages describe casual dine-in seating').

inspectResult.analysis: All 5 problems are the owner-gated conditional routes; zero contract, CRO, evidence or design-token problems. The route-parity check (validatePhase4RoutesMatchPreparedPageMatrix) diffs routesGenerated against every packet pageMatrix entry and carries no awareness of the matrix's own 'conditional' flags/conditions, so a build that faithfully executes the sealed plan's fold-until-confirmed conditions cannot inspect clean. Confirmed empirically before authoring: a probe artifact listing all 46 planned paths inspects clean; the honest 41-route artifact reports exactly these 5. Authoring the 5 pages without owner confirmation would require fabricating halal/craft claims, which the capsule content floor, the sealed Phase 2 conditions and the specialist skill all forbid; editing the validator is forbidden ('never edit validator thresholds/gates to pass').

unresolvedBuildBlockers: SEAL GATE CONFLICT (not a build failure — the build itself passed with exact unconditional-route parity): inspect-artifact/complete-phase route-parity requires all 46 planned routes including the 5 owner-gated conditional pages, while the sealed Phase 2 conditions forbid shipping those pages before owner confirmation. Resolution paths, in preference order: (1) owner answers the halal-certification and craft-claim questionnaire items -> author the 5 gated data entries -> rebuild (45/46 routes) -> re-inspect clean -> seal; (2) runner amends Phase 2 via amend-phase with an audited reason to move the 5 conditional pages out of the sealed matrix until confirmation -> re-prepare Phase 4 packet -> this artifact inspects clean at 41 routes unchanged; (3) workflow owner teaches validatePhase4RoutesMatchPreparedPageMatrix conditional-page awareness (repo change outside this dispatch's authority).
